perturbation.original_text: Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] months from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the term is in years, but the modified text states it is in months. This creates an in-text contradiction due to the different period measurements mentioned. This is in direct conflict with the statement of years, but then there is a second statement saying months. The conflict arises between which term is considered the actual term, since both conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] months from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date', while the changed text says 'Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] months from the Effective Date'. The contradicted text then uses 'months' as the term. The change from 'years' to 'months' in the changed text introduces a direct contradiction, since the contradicted text refers to a different period, but the change makes the two terms now consistent, thus creating a contradiction with the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement may not be assigned without the express written consent of all Parties hereto; provided, however, that any Party may assign this Non-Disclosure Agreement to an Affiliate of such Party without the consent of any other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement may be assigned with the express written consent of all Parties hereto; provided, however, that any Party may not assign this Non-Disclosure Agreement to an Affiliate of such Party without the consent of any other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text presents an in-text contradiction. Initially, it states that the agreement may be assigned with written consent, but then it indicates that it may not be assigned to an affiliate without consent. This contradiction affects the ability to assign the NDA, creating uncertainty about the conditions under which assignment is permissible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement may be assigned with the express written consent of all Parties hereto; provided, however, that any Party may not assign this Non-Disclosure Agreement to an Affiliate of such Party without the consent of any other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text initially allows assignment with consent but then prohibits assignment to affiliates without consent, directly contradicting itself within the same clause. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, making it unclear under what conditions the NDA can be assigned. The Changed text is the same as the Contradicted Text, further emphasizing the immediate and irreconcilable contradiction created by the change.

perturbation.original_text: Any consent or waiver of compliance with any provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be effective only if in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the Party purported to be bound thereby, and then such consent or waiver shall be effective only in the specific instance and for the specific purpose for which it is given.
perturbation.changed_text: Any consent or waiver of compliance with any provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be effective if in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the Party purported to be bound thereby, and then such consent or waiver shall be effective in every instance for the all purposes for which it is given.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the constraint to "in every instance for all the purposes for which it is given" in Section 16, it now directly contradicts the original intention for waivers to only be for a "specific instance and for the specific purpose for which it is given". This affects all sections, and now creates uncertainty, for if the waiver is in effect for all purposes, it undermines the overall agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any consent or waiver of compliance with any provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be effective if in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the Party purported to be bound thereby, and then such consent or waiver shall be effective in every instance for the all purposes for which it is given. No failure or delay by any Party in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial waiver thereof preclude any other exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the clause to state that waivers are effective 'in every instance for all the purposes for which it is given', which directly contradicts the original intention of waivers being effective only in 'the specific instance and for the specific purpose for which it is given.' The contradicted text includes this modified clause, which conflicts with the concept that waivers should be limited, as the original text specified. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
